findings.title: US-Iran ceasefire announcement rallies equities, crashes oil prices
findings.summary: US and Iran agreed to two-week ceasefire contingent on Strait of Hormuz reopening, driving S&P 500 +2.5%, Nasdaq +2.8%, and WTI crude -15% to $96/bbl. This eases immediate energy supply shock risks amid active tariff regime, but fragile deadline risks episodic reversal.
findings.claim_excerpt: U.S.-Iran ceasefire... S&P 500 up 2.5% and the Nasdaq gaining 2.8%. WTI crude down roughly 15% and closing around $96 per barrel.
findings.signal_type_basis: Episodic geopolitical de-escalation tied to 8pm ET deadline, noise unless sustained 3+ weeks.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Short-term relief in energy prices reduces inflationary pressure on tariff-exposed sectors, but ceasefire fragility caps macro impact.
findings.confidence_basis: Direct market data from multiple recaps with consistent price moves.

sources_cited.excerpt: Markets rally on U.S.-Iran ceasefire – S&P 500 up 2.5%, WTI down 15% to $96.
Crude oil prices soared... equity prices fell modestly amid Middle East conflict.

search_queries_run: US Iran ceasefire Strait of Hormuz oil prices April 2026
WTI crude price change past 48 hours
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Two sources corroborate ceasefire announcement, equity rally, and oil plunge with specific figures.

findings.episodic_reason: Tied to two-week ceasefire with 8pm ET deadline risk.

cross_monitor_relevance.erm: Reduces AI capex energy cost risks short-term.
cross_monitor_relevance.scem: Eases commodity supply stress from Middle East.

findings.review_reason: Assess if ceasefire holds beyond deadline for potential CC upgrade.

findings.title: 10Y Treasury yield falls to 4.29% post-ceasefire
findings.summary: US 10-year Treasury yield declined to 4.29% and 2-year to 3.79% as risk-off reversed on ceasefire news. Reflects reduced inflation fears from energy shock, providing tactical bond relief amid tariff-driven yield pressures.
findings.claim_excerpt: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield declined to around 4.29%, while the 2-year yield closed near 3.79%.
findings.signal_type_basis: Reactive to single-day geopolitical news, not sustained trend.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Temporary bond rally eases financial conditions but vulnerable to tariff inflation persistence.
findings.confidence_basis: Precise yield levels reported consistently across market recaps.

sources_cited.excerpt: Sharp increase in yields on US bonds... reflected investor concern about inflation.

search_queries_run: US bond yields Middle East ceasefire
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Sources align on yield decline post-ceasefire amid prior inflation fears.

findings.review_reason: Monitor for sustainment beyond episodic reversal.

findings.title: WTI oil rebounds toward $99/bbl amid Hormuz uncertainty
findings.summary: WTI crude rebounded toward $99 per barrel as Strait of Hormuz disruptions persist despite ceasefire. Signals fragile de-escalation with ongoing supply risks amplifying tariff input cost pressures.
findings.claim_excerpt: In energy markets, WTI oil rebounded toward $99 per barrel as disruptions persist in the Strait of Hormuz.
findings.signal_type_basis: Ongoing disruptions post-ceasefire indicate medium-term supply risks.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Elevated oil supports tariff shock inflation channel into manufacturing.
findings.confidence_basis: Single source on rebound level, corroborated by prior plunge context.

sources_cited.excerpt: WTI oil rebounded toward $99 per barrel as disruptions persist.

search_queries_run: WTI oil price Strait of Hormuz April 9 2026
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: One direct source; context from multi-source ceasefire coverage supports.

cross_monitor_relevance.erm: Sustained high energy costs pressure AI capex.
cross_monitor_relevance.scem: Commodity stress from Mideast amid tariffs.

findings.review_reason: Track Hormuz status for CC to TS downgrade.

findings.title: US services PMI contracts to 49.8 amid war-tariff strain
findings.summary: S&P Global US services PMI fell to 49.8 in March, first contraction since 2023, due to rising prices and uncertainty from Middle East war plus tariffs. Indicates cyclical softening in non-goods economy.
findings.claim_excerpt: US services PMI fell from 51.7 to 49.8... buckling under rising prices and uncertainty, as war exacerbates tariff concerns.
findings.signal_type_basis: PMI sub-50 signals medium-term activity decline under dual shocks.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Tariff-war combo accelerates US cyclical peak, pressuring consumer sectors.
findings.confidence_basis: Specific PMI data with analytical context.

sources_cited.excerpt: PMI for US services fell to 49.8... strain of rising prices and war/tariffs.
Markets rally... following ceasefire amid prior conflict fears.

search_queries_run: US services PMI March 2026 S&P Global
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: PMI figure precise; tariff-war linkage multi-source.

cross_monitor_relevance.scem: EM consumer parallels via US demand slowdown.

findings.review_reason: Confirm if contraction sustains for CC validation.

findings.title: China 10Y yield falls as US/EU/JP bonds rise on inflation fears
findings.summary: China 10Y bond yield declined modestly since Mideast conflict start, unlike rising yields in US/Europe/Japan due to inflation/monpol concerns. Highlights China safe-haven appeal amid tariff decoupling.
findings.claim_excerpt: China 10-year bond yield fallen modestly... exception to sharp yield rises in US/EU/JP on inflation fears.
findings.signal_type_basis: Divergent yield trends reflect structural safe-haven shift under tariffs.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Tariff regime boosts China bond demand, pressuring G7 yields higher.
findings.confidence_basis: Single source on divergence; corroborated by broader yield context.

sources_cited.excerpt: China yield fallen modestly vs rising US/EU/JP yields.

search_queries_run: China 10Y bond yield vs US April 2026
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: One source; aligns with tariff false-positive discipline on dollar flows.

cross_monitor_relevance.esa: EU yield pressure amid US decoupling.

findings.review_reason: Track for SA upgrade if divergence widens.

findings.title: ECB rate hikes priced for 2026 on Eurozone inflation acceleration
findings.summary: Eurozone inflation accelerates, with futures pricing 1-2 ECB benchmark hikes by year-end. Responds to energy shocks and tariff passthrough, diverging from US path.
findings.claim_excerpt: Futures market pricing strong likelihood ECB raises rate one or two times before end of 2026.
findings.signal_type_basis: Monpol shift expectations over weeks/months from inflation data.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Tariff-energy inflation forces ECB tightening, widening US-EU policy gap.

sources_cited.excerpt: Eurozone inflation accelerates... ECB to raise rates 1-2 times.

search_queries_run: ECB rate hike expectations Eurozone inflation April 2026
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Sources support inflation driver and market pricing.

cross_monitor_relevance.esa: Active ECB tightening amid US coercion.

findings.title: US dollar weakens vs majors post-ceasefire
findings.summary: USD weakened against major currencies following ceasefire-driven risk-on. Counters tariff risk-off dollar strength expectation.
findings.claim_excerpt: The U.S. dollar weakened against major currencies.
findings.signal_type_basis: Episodic reversal of risk-off, per tariff false-positive rules.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Temporary USD ease aids EM but embedded in tariff regime.
findings.confidence_basis: Single mention; context from multi-source risk-on.

sources_cited.excerpt: U.S. dollar weakened against major currencies.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: One source; disciplined per false-positive rules.

findings.review_reason: Check for sustainment vs tariff dollar bias.

below_threshold.reason_excluded: Month-ago data outside 48hr window; no new development.
